candidate_problems.text: How can the conjecture’s central analogy be made precise: in what measurable sense is trust a 'compression layer' or 'epistemic infrastructure,' and when does reduced verification cost genuinely track better coordination rather than hidden fragility or exploitation?

What are the causal mechanisms and thresholds by which local failures of corrigibility—opacity, asymmetric error exposure, signal pollution, delayed repair—propagate into system-wide distrust, and are there identifiable tipping points beyond which trust collapse becomes self-reinforcing?

Which institutional designs can restore trust at scale under anonymity, speed, and inequality without sliding into coercive surveillance or brittle over-proceduralization, and what trade-offs determine whether added legibility improves trust versus merely increasing compliance?
